decisions.decision: Keep the current ProbabilityEdit limiter matrix as the sole regression seam for remaining-quota decrement, `429 rate_limit_exceeded`, `Retry-After`, and per-agent isolation because all protected POST routes still rely on the same shared write-control seam.
alternatives_rejected: Clone the full limiter matrix onto create-market, comment-post, market-resolve, or event-trade without evidence of route-local divergence.
Treat route-local handlers as separate limiter seams even though quota accounting and rate-limit headers remain centralized.

decisions.decision: Treat this execution step as preservation-only: maintain the current ProbabilityEdit-vs-breadth split in the tests, and only widen cross-route limiter assertions if execution proves the shared seam is no longer representative.
alternatives_rejected: Preemptively add duplicate decrement, exhaustion, or per-agent-isolation assertions on other protected POST routes.
Reshape the task into broader route-local limiter coverage despite the current shared-seam evidence.

decisions.decision: Keep the work within one worker because the relevant backend seam, existing test inventory, and focused verification path are already known from the local research.
alternatives_rejected: Spawn a child task to re-audit backend route handling.
Split test-preservation and backend-fix exploration into parallel workers.

steps.task: Re-audit the protected-write coverage in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` and confirm the current shape still matches the researched contract: ProbabilityEdit owns the decrement/`429`/`Retry-After`/per-agent-isolation matrix, while create-market, comment-post, market-resolve, and event-trade remain first-success breadth checks.

If the test shape drifted, update the affected tests to restore ProbabilityEdit as the sole limiter matrix and keep the other protected POST routes free of duplicate cross-route exhaustion assertions.

Only if the preserved tests expose a real regression, patch the shared protected-write seam in `backend/server.py` so the centralized remaining-quota decrement, `429`, `Retry-After`, and per-agent isolation behavior is restored without introducing route-specific limiter logic.

Run the focused Bayes market auth/rate-limit tests that exercise the ProbabilityEdit limiter matrix and the protected-route breadth checks, and verify the results do not justify cloning the limiter matrix onto another route.
Review the diff to ensure the step stayed preservation-only, then stage and commit the task work once the focused verification passes.
